_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 2-3 Burns (Revised 05/22/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan Trauma and Environmental Protocol Section 2-3: Burns — MDHHS Approved 5/22/23, Reviewed 2023. Sources: NASEMO (2016); American Burn Association (2022) Guidelines for Burn Patient Referral.

sections.title: Burn Severity Definitions
points: SUPERFICIAL — NOT counted in TBSA: dry, red, easily blanching, sometimes painful (e.g., sunburn)
SUPERFICIAL PARTIAL THICKNESS — counted in TBSA: moist, red, blanching, blisters, very painful
DEEP PARTIAL THICKNESS — counted in TBSA: drier, more pale, less blanching, less pain
FULL THICKNESS — counted in TBSA: dry, leathery texture, variable color (white, brown, black), loss of pin prick sensation
NOTE: When calculating TBSA do NOT include superficial burns (erythematous tissue)

sections.title: General Treatment
points: Follow General Pre-Hospital Care-Treatment Protocol
Pediatric patients (< 14 years of age): utilize MI MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage; when unavailable use pediatric dosing listed within protocol
If evidence of possible airway burn: consider proactive airway management per Airway Management-Procedure Protocol
Administer 100% oxygen to all patients rescued from a confined space fire (building, automobile) regardless of pulse oximetry reading
Determine burn extent and severity (rule of nines, or palm = 1%)
Keep patient warm and avoid hypothermia
Assess and treat for associated injuries
If burns associated with unconsciousness, respiratory burns, or cyanide poisoning: refer to Cyanide Exposure-Special Operations Protocol

sections.title: Thermal Burns
points: Stop the burning process — remove smoldering and non-adherent clothing
Consider potential for secondary contamination
Assess and treat associated trauma
Remove any constricting items
Cover burns with dry clean dressings to prevent hypothermia

sections.title: Chemical Burns
points: Protect personnel from contamination — identify chemical agent when possible
Remove all clothing and constricting items
Decontaminate patient prior to transport, brushing off dry chemicals prior to irrigation — refer to Hazardous Contaminated Patient-Special Operations
Evaluate for systemic symptoms which might be caused by chemical contamination
Notify receiving hospital of possible chemical contamination
Cover burned area in clean, dry dressing for transport

sections.title: Electrical Injury
points: Protect rescuers from live electric wires
When energy source is removed, remove patient from electrical source
Treat associated injuries; provide spinal precautions per Spinal Injury Assessment-Treatment Protocol when indicated
Assess and treat contact wound(s)
Monitor patient ECG for possible arrhythmias — treat per specific arrhythmia protocol (Specialist skill)

sections.title: For All Types of Burns
points: Obtain vascular access if indicated for pain management or fluid therapy per Vascular Access and IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol (Specialist skill)
For patients with hypotension: administer LR (NS if LR not available) IV/IO fluid bolus (Specialist skill); Adults: up to 1 liter; Pediatrics: up to 20 mL/kg
If patient remains hypotensive: consider other underlying causes and contact Medical Control prior to further fluid resuscitation

For non-superficial burns WITHOUT hypotension and BSA > 10% deep partial thickness (second degree) OR any full thickness (third degree): administer fluids by age — < 1 year: contact Medical Control; 1–5 years: 125 mL/hour; 6–13 years: 250 mL/hour; ≥ 14 years: 500 mL/hour
Administer analgesic medication per Pain Management-Procedure Protocol

sections.title: Transport Considerations
points: Follow local MCA Transport Protocol
If severe airway/breathing compromise that cannot be managed: transport to the closest facility
Burn patients that also meet field trauma triage criteria: transport to closest appropriate trauma facility per MCA Transport Protocol

Consider transport directly to burn center if: full thickness burns; partial thickness ≥ 10% TBSA; any deep partial or full thickness burns involving face, hands, genitalia, feet, perineum, or over any joints; all patients with suspected inhalation injury; circumferential burns; all chemical injuries; all high voltage (≥ 1,000V) electrical injuries; lightning injury
Consider air ambulance for long transport times, pain control requiring deep sedation, and airway concerns that might necessitate advanced airway management

key_drugs.name: LR or NS IV/IO Fluid Bolus (Hypotension)
key_drugs.dose: Adults: up to 1 liter; Pediatrics: up to 20 mL/kg
key_drugs.contraindications: Persistent hypotension despite initial bolus — contact Medical Control
key_drugs.notes: Prefer LR; use NS if LR not available. Contact Medical Control if hypotension persists.

key_drugs.name: LR or NS Maintenance Rate (Non-Hypotensive Burns)
key_drugs.dose: BSA > 10% deep partial or any full thickness, no hypotension: < 1 year — contact Medical Control; 1–5 years: 125 mL/hr; 6–13 years: 250 mL/hr; ≥ 14 years: 500 mL/hr
key_drugs.indication: Significant burns without hypotension requiring maintenance fluid
key_drugs.contraindications: Superficial burns only; patient is hypotensive (use bolus protocol instead)
key_drugs.notes: Rate-based maintenance — not bolus. Contact Medical Control for < 1 year patients.

deterioration_flags: Airway burn suspected (facial burns, singed eyebrows/nasal hairs, hoarse voice, carbonaceous sputum) — proactive airway management; transport immediately
Confined space fire exposure — 100% O2 regardless of SpO2; consider CO poisoning and cyanide exposure
Hypotension — initiate fluid bolus (Specialist skill); consider other traumatic causes; contact Medical Control if not responding
Electrical injury with ECG changes — continuous cardiac monitoring; treat dysrhythmia per appropriate protocol
scope_notes: Burn assessment, dressing, cooling/thermal management: all levels
Cardiac monitoring for electrical injury: Specialist skill
Advanced airway management: refer to Airway Management-Procedure Protocol for scope
